content.claim: Poland delayed tracking from age 15 to age 16 (1999 reform), resulting in measured improvements: student outcomes improved above OECD average, employment probability increased by ~3 percentage points, and earnings increased by ~4%
content.context: Fresh live research through the International comparison lens
limitations: Poland's reform occurred in different economic and governance context than Hungary
Temporal confounding: improvements could reflect broader economic or policy changes in post-1999 Poland
Reform was substantially reversed after 2016, suggesting measured benefits did not guarantee political durability
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
